TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua ti Valuliaw, neka nu kina, neka nu kama, neka nu kaka.
S.PHON: izua ti vaɭuɭiaw, nəka nu kina, nəka nu kama, nəka nu kaka.
S.FORM: izua ti Valuliaw, neka nu kina, neka nu kama, neka nu kaka.
S.PHON: izua ti vaɭuɭiaw, nəka nu kina, nəka nu kama, nəka nu kaka.
S.TRANSL: There was a person called Valuliaw, with no mother, father or siblings.

S.FORM: izua za ramaɫeng a vavayan alapen ti Valuliaw tu vuvu.
S.PHON: izua za ramaʎəŋ a vavajan aɭapən ti vaɭuɭiaw tu vuvu.
S.FORM: izua za ramaljeng a vavayan alapen ti Valuliaw tu vuvu.
S.PHON: izua za ramaʎəŋ a vavajan aɭapən ti vaɭuɭiaw tu vuvu.
S.TRANSL: An old woman took Valuliaw as her grandchild.

S.FORM: nu vaik i vavua, sikim tua nakuyakuya nua qaliqali.
S.PHON: nu vaik i vavua, sikim tua nakujakuja nua qaɭiqaɭi.
S.FORM: nu vaik i vavua, sikim tua nakuyakuya nua qaliqali.
S.PHON: nu vaik i vavua, sikim tua nakujakuja nua qaɭiqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: When they went to the fields, others chose for them the bad land (or others searched out the bad (?unused and therefore fertile) land.
When they went to the fields, they went only for one day at a time.

S.FORM: ɫakua nu patjara kivaqu anga, tjaɫalialiavan a vaqu nuaza marevuvu.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu pacara kivaqu aŋa, caʎaɭiaɭiavan a vaqu nuaza marəvuvu.
S.FORM: ljakua nu patjara kivaqu anga, tjaljalialiavan a vaqu nuaza marevuvu.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu paʦara kivaqu aŋa, ʦaʎaɭiaɭiavan a vaqu nuaza marəvuvu.
S.TRANSL: But when it came to millet harvest time, this grandmother and grandchild pair had the most millet.

S.FORM: nu qemaɫup, sikim tua tseva tua qiɫu na karang nua qaliqali.
S.PHON: nu qəmaʎup, sikim tua ʦəva tua qiʎu na karaŋ nua qaɭiqaɭi.
S.FORM: nu qemaljup, sikim tua ceva tua qilju na karang nua qaliqali.
S.PHON: nu qəmaʎup, sikim tua ʦəva tua qiʎu na karaŋ nua qaɭiqaɭi.
S.TRANSL: When they went hunting, others chose for him the ravines and lynx trails (or others searched the ravines and lynx trails).

S.FORM: ɫakua nu maselem saka uri sema umaq anga, ti Valuliaw a liaw a inalap.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu masəɭəm saka uri səma umaq aŋa, ti vaɭuɭiaw a ɭiaw a inaɭap.
S.FORM: ljakua nu maselem saka uri sema umaq anga, ti Valuliaw a liaw a inalap.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu masəɭəm saka uri səma umaq aŋa, ti vaɭuɭiaw a ɭiaw a inaɭap.
S.TRANSL: But when it was dark and they were going back home, Valuliaw had caught the most.

S.FORM: aitsu a ti Valuliaw avan anga nu kakiɫuanan nua Payuan nu temulu tua aɫak.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ti vaɭuɭiaw avan aŋa nu kakiʎuanan nua pajuan nu təmuɭu tua aʎak.
S.FORM: aicu a ti Valuliaw avan anga nu kakiljuanan nua Payuan nu temulu tua aljak.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ti vaɭuɭiaw avan aŋa nu kakiʎuanan nua pajuan nu təmuɭu tua aʎak.
S.TRANSL: When Paiwan people teach their children, it is this Valuliaw who is held out as a model.
